英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾培列索普
zh.lifeSummary: 當Edward Clay於1650年3月在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾培列索普受洗時，他的父親Sir John Gloucester Claye IV124歲，母親Elizabeth Bridges123歲。他於大約1582年與Mary Ann Carlton在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。

英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾培列索普
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Edward Clay于1650年3月在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾培列索普受洗时，他的父亲Sir John Gloucester Claye IV124岁，母亲Elizabeth Bridges123岁。他于大约1582年与Mary Ann Carlton在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Clay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Midlands and Yorkshire): from Old English clǣg ‘clay’, applied as a topographic name for someone who lived in an area of clay soil, a habitational name for someone who lived in a district known as (the) Clay, such as the one in east Notinghamshire, or as a metonymic occupational name for a worker in a clay pit (see Clayman ).

History: The relatively common English name Clay had several American forebears in the 18th century. Henry Clay, born in Hanover, VA, in 1777, secretary of state for President John Quincy Adams, was descended from English ancestors who came to VA shortly after the founding of Jamestown. The revolutionary war officer Joseph Clay, also a member of the Continental Congress, was a native of Yorkshire, England, who emigrated to GA in 1760 and was a founder of the University of Georgia.

variants.name: Klee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ernst, Hans, Hellmut, Helmut, Horst, Kurt, Manfred, Otmar, Otto, Wolfgang.
German: possibly a nickname for a clover farmer, from Klee ‘clover’. Compare Clay and Clee .

variants.name: Clayman
meanings: English: from Middle English cleymann, from cley ‘clay’ (see Clay ) + mann ‘man’, an occupational name for one who prepared clay for use in brick making or pottery, or for a plasterer (see Clare 3).
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) Kleimann, or of Jewish Kleiman , Klayman ‘small man’.

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Edward Clay was christened in March 1650, in Perlethorpe, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir John Gloucester Claye IV, was 124 and his mother, Elizabeth Bridges, was 123. He married Mary Ann Carlton about 1582, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edward Clay a été baptisé en mars 1650, à Perlethorpe, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir John Gloucester Claye IV, avait 124 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Bridges, avait 123 ans. Il a épousé Mary Ann Carlton environ 1582, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Als Edward Clay im März 1650 in Perlethorpe, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom getauft wurde, war sein Vater Sir John Gloucester Claye IV 124 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Bridges 123. Er hat um 1582 in England, United Kingdom Mary Ann Carlton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Clay è stato battezzato nel marzo 1650 nel Perlethorpe, Nottinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir John Gloucester Claye IV, aveva 124 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Bridges, aveva 123 anni. Lui sposato Mary Ann Carlton circa 1582, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia.

ja.lifeSummary: Edward Clayが1650年3月にイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアペルレソルペで洗礼を受けた時，父Sir John Gloucester Claye IVは124歳，母Elizabeth Bridgesは123歳であった。約1582年にイギリスイギリスでMary Ann Carltonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Edward Clay(이)가 1650년 3월 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 p를레소에서 침례를 받았을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Gloucester Claye IV(은)는 124세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Bridges(은)는 123세였다. 그는 대략 1582년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Mary Ann Carlton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

Perlethorpe, Snotengaham, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Clay foi batizado em março de 1650, em Perlethorpe, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir John Gloucester Claye IV, tinha 124 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Bridges, tinha 123 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary Ann Carlton aproximadamente 1582, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edward Clay был крещен марта 1650 г. в местности пирлетаурп, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir John Gloucester Claye IV было 124 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Bridges, 123. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Ann Carlton около 1582 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Edward Clay se bautizó en marzo de 1650, en Perlethorpe, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir John Gloucester Claye IV, tenía 124 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Bridges, tenía 123 años. Se casó con Mary Ann Carlton aproximadamente 1582, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija.
